title: ESIC Registration Online in India | Process, Cost & Consultant

hero.headline: Get ESIC Registration with Complete Compliance
hero.subtext: Secure your team’s welfare and avoid penalties. Professionals Law handles everything from filing to follow-up.

mainHeading: ESIC Employer Registration Online Service in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Employees State Insurance Corporation Registration in India is a self-financing social security and health insurance scheme for Indian workers. It is enforced by the Ministry of Labour and Employment of the Government of India and applies to establishments employing 10 or more persons whose monthly remuneration does not exceed ₹21,000. Under the scheme, insured employees and their dependants are provided with medical, maternity, disablement and dependent benefits. Professionals Law help businesses get the ESIC Registration quickly and also ensure that there is full compliance with the return filing and payment of contribution.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: ESIC Registration enable the business organization as it has to fulfill its statutory duty of taking care of their employee welfare. It not only is about being legally compliant, but it lends the protection of health and insurance to the employees and their family members. Professionals Law ensures businesses are compliant and receive an easy ESIC journey.

body.title: Medical Benefits to Employees
body.description: Employees and their families receive free or subsidized medical care through a network of ESI hospitals and dispensaries.

body.title: Maternity & Sickness Benefits
body.description: Insured women employees are entitled to maternity leave with pay, while temporary sickness benefits cover up to 91 days in a year.

body.title: Disability & Dependent Support
body.description: Financial support is offered in case of employment-related injuries, permanent disability or death of an employee.

body.title: Legal Compliance and Reputation
body.description: ESIC compliance enhances a company’s credibility and demonstrates its commitment to employee welfare and statutory obligations.

body.title: Cash Benefits during Inability to Work
body.description: Employees receive financial assistance when they are unable to work due to illness, injury or maternity, ensuring income continuity.

criteria: Establishments with 10 or more employees (in some states, the threshold is 20).
Employees earning up to ₹21,000 per month (₹25,000 for employees with disabilities).
Applicable to factories, shops, hotels, cinemas, educational institutions and other commercial establishments.

documents: PAN Card of the business entity
List of all employees with details (name, DOB, salary, joining date, etc.)
Bank account details of the company
Employee ID proof and address proof
Professionals Law ensures all documentation is accurately compiled and submitted to prevent delays or rejections.

body.description: Professionals Law reviews your business type and employee strength to determine ESIC applicability.
All necessary documents are gathered, verified and formatted as per ESIC portal standards.
Application is filed on the ESIC portal using the employer's details.
On approval, a 17-digit unique employer code is issued.
Employees are registered and issued Individual Insurance Numbers (IP numbers).
Professionals Law assists with monthly contributions, return filings and audits.

penalties.heading: Penalties
body: Non-compliance with ESIC provisions may attract:
A penalty of ₹5,000 for delayed registration.
Interest and damages on delayed contributions ranging from 5% to 25%.
Legal action including imprisonment for habitual defaulters.
Professionals Law helps clients avoid such liabilities through timely registration and dedicated compliance support.

body.title: Standard Processing Time
body.description: The ESIC registration process usually takes 5 to 7 working days, depending on the completeness of documents and portal responses.

body.question: Is ESIC registration mandatory?
body.answer: Yes, it is mandatory for establishments employing 10 or more employees (in some states, 20).

body.question: What is the contribution rate for ESIC?
body.answer: Employers contribute 3.25% and employees contribute 0.75% of the gross wages.

body.question: Can a company voluntarily register under ESIC?
body.answer: Yes, companies with fewer employees can opt-in voluntarily to provide benefits to their workforce.

body.question: Are all employees eligible under ESIC?
body.answer: Only employees earning up to ₹21,000 per month are covered (₹25,000 for disabled employees).

body.question: Is ESIC applicable to contract employees?
body.answer: Yes, even contractual and casual employees are covered under ESIC if the establishment meets eligibility.

body.question: What are the employer’s responsibilities post-registration?
body.answer: Employers must file regular ESI returns and pay monthly contributions on time.

body.question: Can Professionals Law handle post-registration compliance as well?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law offers end-to-end ESIC compliance services including filing, employee updates and audit assistance.

body.question: What if an employer does not deduct or deposit ESI contributions?
body.answer: The employer may face penalties, interest charges and even prosecution under the ESI Act.

body.question: Is ESIC registration permanent?
body.answer: Yes, but the employer must keep records updated and comply with periodic filings to keep the registration active.
